prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Well, sweetie, from what I understand, the CPU is like the brain of the computer. It’s a tiny little chip that reads lots of small instructions and tells the rest of the machine what to do, very quickly. When you click a button or open a game, the CPU does the thinking to make it happen, working with the computer’s memory to keep track of things. I picture it like a very fast librarian or a busy orchestra conductor keeping everyone in time. I don’t know all the fancy details, but without it, the computer would just sit there like an unplugged toaster.
